home cv blog dev-projects

Education

June 2018 - May 2022 Oregon Health & Science University, Portland, OR

Studied applications of public omics data for cross-study analyses, experiment planning, and independent validation of prior findings. Published the recountmethylation Bioconductor package and affiliated compilations of over 60k DNA methylation array samples from the HM450K and EPIC platforms, mined from the Gene Expression Omnibus. Studied the reliability of retained intron-calling software developed for short-read RNA-seq using sample-matched PacBio long-read RNA-seq data in stem cell and whole blood. Completed 3 first-author manuscripts (1 published, 1 in review, 1 submitted), and 2 co-author contributions (both published).

  • Dissertation: “Applications of public omics data”
  • Ph.D. Computational Biology
  • Advisor: Prof. Abhinav Nellore
  • Department of Biomedical Engineering
  • Oregon Health & Science University
  • Portland, OR

2007-2011 Reed College, Portland, OR

Rigorous liberal arts and humanities curriculum (e.g. no grade inflation); coursework concentration in STEM disciplines, especially population genetics and ecology. Conducted novel, collaborative herpetological research of Rana aurora native frogs as a Fischer Memorial Fellow. Completed a study of population genetic diversity decline in captive A. burtoni fishes, using microsatellites, for Senior year thesis.

  • Thesis: “Small Fish in a Small Pond: Observed Declines in Genetic Diversity Across Successive Generations of a Captive” Astatotilapia Burtoni” Lineage, Using Microsatellite Markers”
  • B.A. Biology
  • Academic Advisor: Prof. Keith Karoly
  • Thesis Advisor: Prof. Suzy Renn
  • Biology Department
  • Reed College
  • Portland, OR

Research experience

June 2022 - Current Postdoctoral fellow

Department of Biostatistics Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health Advisor: Prof. Stephanie Hicks

Postdoctoral research fellow developing methods to deconvolve cell content information using matched single-cell and bulk RNA-seq data from human brain. Comparative analyses of normal and diseased brain across public datasets. Development of an R/Bioconductor package to aid novel deconvolution of cell content from bulk RNA-seq data in brain and other tissues. Research projects to be conducted in collaboration with members of the Lieber Institute.

  • Single-cell RNA sequencing
  • 10x Genomics Visium image analysis
  • RNAscope analysis
  • Cell type deconvolution methods
  • Feature selection
  • Dimensionality reduction
  • Normal versus diseased tissue
  • Differential cell content analyses

2015-2018 Data Analyst Assistant

Epigenetics analyst and co-author contributor in Grady Lab, Clinical Research Division, Fred Hutch, Seattle, WA

  • Epigenetic modeling
  • Epigenetic drift and aging
  • Biomarker discovery
  • Gastrointestinal oncology
  • Software utilities development
  • Manuscript and grant writing

May - September 2015 Bioinformatics Intern

Interned with Grady Lab to study epigenetics and gastrointestinal cancer risk, Clinical Research Division, Fred Hutch, Seattle, WA

  • Epigenetic modeling
  • Colorectal cancer biomarkers
  • R programming
  • Results visualization

January - October 2013 Laboratory Assistant

Clinical genomics and epidemiological data analysis, Jarvik Lab at University of Washington Medical Genetics, Seattle, WA

  • Genome-wide association studies (GWAS)
  • Manuscript contribution, co-authorship
  • Gene-environment interactions
  • Population genetics
  • Lipids and cholesterol

Publications

See my full publications at Google Scholar

Software

  • 2020 recountmethylation, Bioconductor/R package

Utilities to obtain, query, and analyze cross-study compilations of DNAm array datasets from the Gene Expression Omnibus. Includes vignettes providing background about data types, reproductions of published analyses, and implementations of published software to perform power analyses, predict genetic ancestry from DNA methylation array data, and more.

  • 2021 recountmethylation_instance, Snakemake workflow

Synchronize public DNAm array data. This resource provides a full Snakemake workflow to download, harmonize, and compile IDAT-derived DNAm and SOFT-derived sample metadata from GEO experiments.

Awards

May 2019 Graduate Student Travel Award

Computer science and informatics conference hosted by the Department of Computer Science, University of Pittsburg. Presented a poster introducing meta-analysis of DNA methylation array data and variation in the Gene Expression Omnibus (Nellore Lab). MOMACs Modeling the World’s Systems, Washington, DC.

May 2016 - May 2017 SAS-BWF Fellow

Research collaboration hosted by the SAS Institute and Burroughs Wellcome Fund. Collaborations with SAS JMP Genomics team, researched epigenetic instability in colorectal cancers and matched normal tissues (Grady Lab). Fred Hutch, Seattle, WA.

May - September 2010 Fischer Memorial Fellow

Field survey and habitat study of the native, at-risk frog species Rana aurora . Performed amphibian ecology literature review, reared tadpoles, and constructed breeding habitat. Authored and coauthored research reports (Kaplan Lab). Reed College, Portland, OR.

Presentations

Summer 2021 Software workshop/demo, Bioc2021

Sean K. Maden, Reid F. Thompson, Kasper D. Hansen, Abhinav Nellore. Accessing, querying, and analyzing public DNAm array data compilations with recountmethylation. Virtual/remote presentation for the Bioc2021 conference. Visit the demo’s repo for the lecture recording, presentation materials, code vignette, cheat sheet, and wiki.

December 2019 Poster/abstract, CEEHRC/IHEC Annual Meeting

Sean K. Maden, Kasper D. Hansen, Abhi Nellore. Human methylome variation across Infinium 450K raw data on the Gene Expression Omnibus. Canadian Epigenetics, Environment and Health Research Consortium (CEEHRC) Network and International Human Epigenome Consortium (IHEC) Annual Meeting in Banff, AB, Canada.

May 2019 Poster/abstract, MOMACS

Sean K. Maden, Kasper D. Hansen, Abhinov Nellore. Comprehensive Reanalysis of DNA Methylation Array Data. MOMACS Modeling the World’s Systems Conference in Washington, DC.

Skills and expertise

  • R/Bioconductor (recountmethylation)
  • Python for analysis (pandas, numpy, scipy)
  • R for data science (data.table, dplyr)
  • Plots for publication (ggplot2, UpsetR)
  • Data dashboards (shiny and plotly)
  • Version control (git, GitHub)
  • Public omics data mining (GEO, SRA, Entrez programming utilities)
  • Omics consortia (TCGA, CCLE, GTEx)
